title: She survived a brain tumor. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tracy Haisley, a 50-year-old from Columbia, South Carolina, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving a brain tumor.
Haisley underwent brain surgery and three months of rehabilitation, transforming a life-changing challenge into marathon motivation.
She aims to raise awareness for public libraries by running with the Boston Public Library team, reflecting her long-term advocacy.

title: What Jaylen Brown said of Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown praised Jayson Tatum's return to the Celtics after recovering from a ruptured Achilles injury.
Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists in a 120-100 win over the Dallas Mavericks.
Brown emphasized the team’s focus on overcoming challenges and achieving a deep playoff run with Tatum's return.

title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg expressed his commitment to the Mavericks, despite growing up as a Boston Celtics fan in Maine.
Flagg, drafted No. 1 by the Mavericks, has a rookie deal with club options through 2029, delaying free agency until 2030.
During his first NBA game at TD Garden, Flagg enjoyed the homecoming experience despite the Mavericks' 20-point loss to the Celtics.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics lineup after a ten-month absence, playing 27 minutes with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists.
Tatum missed his initial shots but quickly found his rhythm, contributing significantly to the Celtics' win over the Dallas Mavericks.
Tatum's return aligns with the team's preparation for the playoffs, as Boston holds second place in the Eastern Conference standings.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup without minutes restriction against the Mavericks after a 10-month injury hiatus.
Tatum contributed 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in the Celtics' 120-100 win over the Mavericks.
Nikola Vucevic is expected to miss a month after fracturing his ring finger, requiring surgery.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney chose not to trade future draft assets for short-term roster improvements.
Boston made minor AHL-level trades but retained key players and future draft picks.
Bruins focus on rebuilding strategy, anticipating improved roster with prospects and cap space.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby is reportedly being traded from the Las Vegas Raiders to the Baltimore Ravens for two first-round picks.
Crosby desired to join the Patriots and head coach Mike Vrabel, according to The Athletic’s Dianna Russini.
The Patriots, with a $60 million cap space, missed matching the Ravens' trade offer for Crosby.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic fractured his right ring finger against the Dallas Mavericks and will be out for a month.
Vucevic suffered the injury in the first half and played just two minutes before leaving the game.
His absence affects Celtics' front court depth, with alternatives including Neemias Queta and rookie Amari Williams.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics lineup 297 days after rupturing his Achilles tendon in 2025.
During his return game, Tatum played six minutes initially, contributing two assists and two rebounds.
Tatum scored his first points of the season in the second quarter, aiding a Celtics team second in the Eastern Conference.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round pick, per ESPN's Adam Schefter.
Trade cannot be official until new league year; Bears need center after Drew Dalman's retirement.
Patriots could replace Bradbury with Jared Wilson, who played center at Georgia in 2024.
